myinfo.name: Jordan Latta

content.alt: A high-resolution profile photo of Jordan Latta.
A high-resolution profile photo of Jordan Latta.

myinfo.tagline: Specializes in real-time rendering, shader development, and optimization with over a decade of self-directed experience in Unity. Check out my work here!
myinfo.aboutDescription: My life is built from an unshakable obsession with how digital worlds are made. At six, Super Mario 64 was the spark. I grew up with its modding scene, teaching myself Game Maker in 2007, spending late nights modeling levels in SketchUp and resolving emulation bugs. In 2015 came Unity3D and finally shaders, which I've spent a decade mastering. Today, my lifelong devotion translates into building ultra-performant rendering systems and effects — all to give players the same spark I first felt.

responsibilities: Owned the design and development of custom GPU-driven rendering systems in C# on Unity's SRP, extending visuals and performance beyond default engine capabilities.
Partnered closely with clients, engineers, artists, and designers to build tools — including a grass creation system and a 2D lighting editor — that streamlined both art and engineering workflows.
Authored and maintained tools, shaders, models, materials, and textures across evolving art styles and shifting platform targets.
Built and documented asset pipelines end-to-end, training peers along the way.

education.degree: B.A. in Games & Interactive Media | Minor in Game Design & Development
